button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to discuss work experience in an interview

1.In my previous role, I was responsible for handling client accounts, which helped me develop relationship management skills.
2.I have worked before but I did not learn anything useful from it.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to connect past experience to a skill

1.My previous job was not relevant to anything I do now.
2.In my previous role, I was responsible for preparing financial reports, which helped me develop analytical thinking skills.

Correct answer: 2
In my previous role, I was responsible for handling client inquiries, which helped me develop strong communication and patience.
In my previous role, I was responsible for coordinating between departments, which helped me develop cross-functional collaboration skills.
In my previous role, I was responsible for managing deadlines and deliverables, which helped me develop strong time management skills.
In my previous role, I was responsible for creating training materials, which helped me develop instructional design skills.
In my previous role, I was responsible for resolving customer complaints, which helped me develop problem-solving and empathy.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. The interviewer asks what tasks you handled in your last job. Share your responsibility and the skill you gained.'

'2. The interviewer asks how your past work experience is relevant to this new role. Share your task and skill.'

'3. The interviewer asks what you learned from your previous position. Share your responsibility and key takeaway.'

'4. The interviewer asks about a challenge you handled in your last role. Share your task and the skill it helped you develop.'

'5. The interviewer asks how your past experience prepared you for this role. Share your previous responsibility and skill.'"
